title: Metadata Editor global data structure (DSD) — create / import
description: Payload for POST /api/data_structures/import_json: create a catalogue global DSD (data_structures + components) and associated global codelists. Root shape matches the study-side DSD envelope: data_structure holds identity fields plus components[]. Optional root-level overwrite and dry_run are read only from this JSON body (not from query strings). overwrite governs destructive updates where the API applies them (matched codelists when inline items are supplied; structure-level behaviour as implemented). dry_run validates without persisting. Each component uses codelist (inline binding) and/or codelist_reference (canonical catalogue identity); when both are present, codelist_reference takes precedence. Cross-field rules are enforced by the API, not only this schema.

codeListItem.description: One code in a global codelist. Maps to codelist_item: code, title (from label), sort_order, parent_id. Description is carried in the payload for documentation or import logic if the persistence layer supports it.

code.title: Code
code.description: Stable code value (unique within the codelist).

label.title: Label
label.description: Display label; import maps to codelist_item.title when persisting.

description.title: Description
description.description: Optional longer text; import may persist if supported or ignore.

parent_id.title: Parent item id
parent_id.description: Optional codelist_item.id of the parent row for hierarchies; use when parent already exists (e.g. second import pass).

codelistBinding.description: Nested codelist binding for a component. Reuse existing catalogue codelist by supplying only 'idno'. Create a new codelist by supplying 'idno' + 'name' (and optional agency/version/description) together with 'items'. When the list already exists and 'items' is present, they are applied only if overwrite=true on the request; otherwise items are ignored with a warning.

idno.title: Codelist idno
idno.description: Catalogue codelist idno. Required to reuse an existing codelist; required when creating a new codelist so studies can reference it stably.

name.title: Codelist maintainable name
name.description: Required when creating a new codelist.

agency.title: Agency
agency.description: Defaults to NADA in API if omitted.

version.title: Version
version.description: Defaults to 1.0 in API if omitted.

items.title: Codelist items
items.description: Codes to load when creating a new codelist (or when overwrite is true on existing).

codelistReference.description: Canonical catalogue codelist identity. When present with codelist on the same component, this reference wins and inline codelist is ignored for binding.

uri.title: Source URI
uri.description: Optional URL for documentation or discovery; not used by import.

dsdComponent.description: One DSD column. For dimension/geography, supply codelist_reference (existing catalogue idno) and/or codelist for inline create/reuse with items.

name.title: Name
name.description: Field name in observations; must not start with underscore.

paired_time_column.title: Paired time_period component name
paired_time_column.description: Name of the sibling component with column_type time_period (e.g. periodicity paired with TIME_PERIOD). Logical reference only.

data_structure.description: Global DSD catalogue identity plus components. idno is the handle for studies (metadata.data_structure_reference).

agency.title: Agency
agency.description: Defaults to NADA in API if omitted.

idno.title: DSD idno
idno.description: Stable catalogue idno; required for JSON import.

components.title: Components
components.description: Ordered DSD columns to create under this structure.

overwrite.description: When true, allows destructive updates for catalogue artefacts touched by this import (e.g. replacing codes on matched codelists when inline items are supplied; structure-related rules as defined by the server). When false, existing codelists are not mutated.

dry_run.description: When true, validate only; do not persist.
